We talked about our vision for the future, right? We talked about the standards that has to be congruent with that vision. Now let's talk about the goals that you set every single day. I want you to start setting your goals on a daily basis. You want to have daily goals, whatever your industry is, whatever you're doing, you want to have a daily goal, something that you can peg. What am I going to try to achieve today? It's a short-term goal and because it's a goal, like every other goal has a date on it, it has a beginning point and an end point. So you set a goal, you put a date on it, right? Now the next step is to identify the people, the groups, the organizations you need to align yourself with. You typically can't accomplish something on your own. You need to help others. You need to get others involved with you, right? Recruit martial resources, right? I want you to also identify whatever skills you need to learn, any skills that you have. Make sure that you're not trying to achieve a goal and you haven't actually done any of the work that's required here, like the training, the knowledge base, you haven't acquired it, that's required to achieve the goal. This is one of the biggest mistakes people make because they go out and they try to achieve something and frankly, they just suck at it. Like, you know, honestly, you're never going to be great as a salesperson if you can't become proficient in closing. Now, just because you become proficient in closing doesn't mean you'll be a great salesperson, right? This is more to it than that. This is the inner game as well, but you have to have the skill to do what you want to do. And that goes for anything in life. So make sure you don't miss that crucial step of identifying any skills gap you might have and then brushing up on your skills, getting the training that you need so you can do, you actually empirically can do what you're trying to do. Where else are we banging your head against the wall and getting nowhere fast, okay? And then the next step is you want to develop a plan, a strategy, a plan of attack. And by the way, this is not a perfect plan. It's going to be the best plan possible based on the information you have, on the resources you have, and your level of expertise. Don't wait to develop a plan, but develop a plan. Have some sort of step-by-step strategy that you're starting with and then take action, massive action. And then you want to have what Dr. Bandler calls from NLP, sensory acuity, to really be watching and analyzing your results very closely. And you see which aspect of what you're doing is working, which aspects are working. And then you want to change up the things that aren't working one at a time. Don't change everything wholesale because you might change things that actually are working. You might be only one thing wrong and you're not getting the result you want because of it. So don't change everything at once, okay? And then you take action again and keep running that loop. And just so you know, and here's one tip before I tell you to go after and really smash it today, is that when you're running that strategy, here's where it breaks down. Take action, analyze your results, and then take action again if you fix something and see what the results are again until you get the result you want, right? Here's where it breaks down. What typically happens is that when you take action and you work at something, you don't get the result you're looking for, you end up getting dejected. You fall into a disempowered state. And what happens is if you end up analyzing your results while you're in a disempowered state, there's like a 95% chance that you're going to end up drawing the wrong conclusions from what you did. In other words, you're not going to look at it objectively and say, okay, here's what was wrong. I didn't study this aspect. What I want to do, my plan was floored in this area. I had bad leads. Maybe I called them the wrong aspect of the market. Maybe I should have, I mailed the wrong time of the day. Maybe my cop, instead of that, you'll say, oh, I suck. This business sucks. You'll come with a disempowered wholesale shock and everything's wrong. I'm stupid. And that's what people miss. They end up trying to analyze their results and fix things while they're feeling disempowered. As human beings, we don't make all good decisions when we're in a disempowered state. So remember your state management. I want you to snap into that peak state, use your anchor, trigger a peak state, and then analyze your results. Remember, you've got to be in that peak state of certainty while you're out there and you're taking action in those blips, those moments where it matters most. You see how everything comes together here? At the end of the day, when it comes to success, it's always about all these different elements I teach, essentially coming together and being used as one cohesive package. That's what success is all about. That's what creates it. And that's what you need to keep brushing up on each individual aspect, one day, one another day. And step by step, you start owning all these skills, all these resources, and bam, eventually you can help but succeed. All right? So remember this. If you go out and you set your daily goals, okay, and then you're going to have your weekly goals as well. I want you to have your monthly goals. Just remember that your goals are merely signposts along the way that tell you whether or not you're heading in the right direction of your vision. Are you moving forward or are you moving to the wrong side? They're not the be all end. They're like, oh my God, I didn't achieve my goal. Oh my God, my life's over. No. Listen, when you're setting goals correctly, very often you're not going to achieve your goal because I want you to stretch. In other words, don't just, you know, and I've said this to you before, the problem with most people is not because they set their goals too high and miss them. The problem is they set their goals too low and they hit them. So if you set your goals too low, yeah, you'll hit them but you end up being just mediocre. The enemy of great is good and good sucks. You need to be great. You need to be extraordinary. So stretch. Remember, you know, if I say put your hand up as high as you possibly can and you do that, right? And I say a bit high, you're like, oh, really? And then you, what the f***, right? Come on. I asked you to put it up as high as you possibly could the first time but there's always that little couple of inches above it. That's what I want you to stretch to. Don't go insane. Don't make something crazy up but stretch yourself on your goals. Don't worry if you don't achieve them. That's not what your emotional investment is. It's in your vision but set them every day and a lot of times you will achieve them but many times you won't. Okay? And it shows you what's right, what's wrong and take action again. You do that. I promise you, you will succeed.
